h1: Benefits for Seniors with Alzheimer's Disease (2026)
introText: Find benefits, financial assistance, and support programs for seniors living with Alzheimer's Disease. Learn about federal programs, Medicaid and Medicare coverage, and caregiving resources.
overview: Alzheimer's Disease affects approximately 6.7 million Americans. Seniors with this condition may qualify for additional benefits and support services at the federal and state levels.

federalPrograms.name: SSDI
federalPrograms.description: Social Security Disability Insurance for people with Alzheimer's Disease who can no longer work

federalPrograms.name: SSI
federalPrograms.description: Supplemental Security Income for low-income individuals with disabilities

medicaidCoverage: Medicaid may cover treatments, medications, home care, and long-term care services for seniors with Alzheimer's Disease. Coverage varies by state.
medicareCoverage: Medicare covers medically necessary treatments for Alzheimer's Disease, including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescription drugs (Part D), and some home health services.
vaBenefits: Veterans with Alzheimer's Disease may qualify for VA disability compensation, health care, Aid & Attendance, and other benefits.

caregiverResources.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program
caregiverResources.description: Information, respite, and support for family caregivers

faqs.question: Can I get disability benefits for Alzheimer's Disease?
faqs.answer: Yes, if Alzheimer's Disease prevents you from working, you may qualify for SSDI or SSI. You'll need medical documentation of your condition.

faqs.question: Does Medicare cover treatment for Alzheimer's Disease?
faqs.answer: Medicare covers medically necessary treatments for Alzheimer's Disease, including doctor visits, hospital care, and prescription medications through Part D.
